title: Ferrofluid Dance
purpose: Shader context pack for ferrofluid simulation — magnetic fluid spike dynamics, field line visualization, and black liquid geometry responding to moving magnetic fields. Designed for RepoScripter Experimental ingestion to blend magnetic-physics aesthetics into generative shader designs.
visual_motifs: jet-black liquid surface with magnetic spike array
field line topology around a dipole magnet
thin bright meniscus glint at spike tips
labyrinthine stripe-domain patterns from rotating in-plane field
spiral vortex arms from rotating magnet
finger instability in Hele-Shaw cell (top-down view)
dendritic magnetite crystal growth following gradient lines
black droplets in amber carrier (inverse ferrofluid)
spike array as cortical column map (neural analog)
techniques: magnetic dipole field calculation (B ∝ r⁻³, cosθ modulation)
Biot-Savart exact current-loop field with elliptic integrals
audio-FFT band modulation of capillary waves
signed-distance surface rendering with edge-glow shading
spike profile sharpening via exponential field-gradient mask
palette: deep subsurface warm highlight (~#6A4A30 at low opacity)
near-black background with subtle blue gradient
optional: gold highlight, rainbow edge, UV-fluorescent variants
motion: Rosensweig spike growth and collapse (fast, violent relaxation)
magnet rotation → spiral arm vortex filaments
magnet oscillation → traveling capillary wave through spike array
multiple magnets → spike competition and field-line interference
Hele-Shaw finger propagation and droplet splitting
labyrinthine wave fronts and magnetic solitons
audio-driven: bass = slow heave, treble = micro-shimmer
biological mode: spike branching, predation, growth, dissolution
geometry: 2D SDF fluid surface (1D height field per column)
dipole field topology: figure-8 lobes, equatorial null
Halbach comb: perfectly periodic unilateral flux
avoid: dry geometric abstraction without physical grounding
purely synthetic noise patterns without fluid physics logic
